info.title: KubernetesResourcesClient
info.description: APIs for K8s inventory reports under Microsoft.KubernetesConfiguration.

get.description: List kubernetesResources containers for the cluster (currently always a single 'default').

parameters.name: clusterRp
parameters.description:
The Kubernetes cluster RP - i.e. Microsoft.ContainerService, Microsoft.Kubernetes,
Microsoft.HybridContainerService, Microsoft.KubernetesConfiguration.

parameters.name: clusterResourceName
parameters.description:
The Kubernetes cluster resource name - i.e. managedClusters, connectedClusters,
provisionedClusters, appliances.

parameters.name: clusterName
parameters.description: The name of the kubernetes cluster.

get.description: Get the kubernetesResources singleton for a cluster.

parameters.name: clusterRp
parameters.description:
The Kubernetes cluster RP - i.e. Microsoft.ContainerService, Microsoft.Kubernetes,
Microsoft.HybridContainerService, Microsoft.KubernetesConfiguration.

parameters.name: clusterResourceName
parameters.description:
The Kubernetes cluster resource name - i.e. managedClusters, connectedClusters,
provisionedClusters, appliances.

parameters.name: clusterName
parameters.description: The name of the kubernetes cluster.

AttachedVolume.description: A volume attached to a Kubernetes Node by the attach/detach controller.

name.description: Name of the attached volume (matches the PersistentVolume name).

devicePath.description: Device path on the node where the volume is attached (e.g., '/dev/sdc').

Capabilities.description: Linux capability set to add to or drop from a container's default capabilities.

ConditionStatus.description: Whether a Kubernetes condition is currently active.

ContainerPortInfo.description: A port exposed by a container.

containerPort.description: Port number exposed inside the container.

hostPort.description: Port number to expose on the host node.

hostIp.description: Host IP to bind the hostPort on.

ContainerProbe.description:
Simplified health probe configuration for a container.
Captures the probe mechanism type and the primary target (path or port).
Detailed timing parameters (initialDelaySeconds, periodSeconds, etc.) can be stored in additionalFields.

port.description: Port number for HTTPGet or TCPSocket probes.

ContainerResourceQuantity.description:
CPU and memory resource quantities for a container.
Values use Kubernetes resource notation: CPU as millicores (e.g., '500m', '2')
and memory as byte-unit strings (e.g., '256Mi', '1Gi').
See: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/

cpu.description: CPU quantity (e.g., '500m' for 500 millicores, '2' for 2 cores).

ContainerResourceRequirements.description: CPU and memory resource declarations for a container.

limits.description: Maximum resources the container may consume.

requests.description: Minimum resources the container requires to be scheduled.

ContainerSecurityContext.description: Security context applied to a single container.

capabilities.description: Linux capability adjustments for this container.

privileged.description: When true, the container runs with full host privileges (root + all Linux capabilities).

readOnlyRootFilesystem.description: Mount the container's root filesystem as read-only when true.

allowPrivilegeEscalation.description: Prevent the container process from gaining more privileges than its parent when false.

runAsNonRoot.description: Require the container to run as a non-root user when true.

runAsUser.description: UID to run the container process as.

runAsGroup.description: GID to run the container process as.

seccompProfile.description: Seccomp profile type applied to this container ('RuntimeDefault', 'Unconfined', or 'Localhost').

ContainerSpec.description: Specification of a single container within a pod.

name.description: Name of the container, unique within the pod.

imagePullPolicy.description: Image pull policy for this container ('Always', 'IfNotPresent', or 'Never').

ports.description: Ports to expose from the container.

env.description: Environment variables to set in the container.

envFrom.description: Sources that populate environment variables in bulk from ConfigMaps or Secrets.

command.description: Command to override the container default ENTRYPOINT.

args.description: Arguments to override the container default CMD.

volumeMounts.description: Volume paths to mount inside the container.

securityContext.description: Security context applied to this container.

livenessProbe.description: Liveness probe that determines when to restart the container.

readinessProbe.description: Readiness probe that determines when the container is ready to accept traffic.

ContainerState.description: Current or previous state of a container; exactly one of the three fields is populated.

running.description: Populated when the container is currently executing.

terminated.description: Populated when the container has exited.

waiting.description: Populated when the container is not yet running.

ContainerStateRunning.description: State of a currently running container.

startedAt.description: UTC timestamp when the container started.

exitCode.description: Exit code of the container process.

signal.description: Signal that caused the container to terminate, if applicable.

startedAt.description: UTC timestamp when the container started its last execution.

finishedAt.description: UTC timestamp when the container terminated.

ContainerStateWaiting.description: State of a container that is not yet running.

reason.description: Machine-readable reason the container is not running (e.g., 'ContainerCreating', 'ImagePullBackOff').

message.description: Human-readable message about the waiting state.

ContainerStatus.description: Runtime status of a single container as reported in the pod status.

ready.description: True when the container has passed its readiness probe.

restartCount.description: Number of times the container has restarted since the pod was started.

image.description: Container image including digest as resolved by the runtime.

started.description: Whether the container has passed its startup probe and is considered started.

lastState.description: Previous termination state of the container; populated after a restart.

EndpointSliceEndpoint.description: A single endpoint within an EndpointSlice (address + readiness + scheduling hints).

addresses.description: IP addresses (IPv4/IPv6) or FQDNs serving this endpoint.

ready.description: True when the endpoint is ready to receive traffic.

nodeName.description: Name of the node hosting this endpoint.

EndpointSlicePort.description: A port entry in an EndpointSlice.

EnvFromSource.description: A source for bulk-importing environment variables into a container from a ConfigMap or Secret.

configMapRef.description: Name of the ConfigMap whose keys are imported as environment variables.

secretRef.description: Name of the Secret whose keys are imported as environment variables.

prefix.description: Optional prefix prepended to each imported environment variable name.

EnvVar.description: An environment variable set in a container.

IngressPath.description: An HTTP path rule within an Ingress host entry, mapping a URL path to a backend Service.

serviceName.description: Name of the backend Service receiving matched traffic.

servicePort.description: Port of the backend Service (numeric string or named port).

IngressRule.description: A host-level routing rule in an Ingress resource.

host.description: Fully qualified domain name or wildcard host (e.g., 'api.example.com', '*.example.com').

paths.description: HTTP path rules mapping URL paths to backend Services.

IngressTls.description: TLS configuration entry for an Ingress resource.

hosts.description: Hostnames covered by this TLS certificate.

secretName.description: Name of the Secret containing the TLS certificate and private key.

KubernetesAnnotation.description: A single Kubernetes annotation (key/value pair for non-identifying auxiliary metadata).

KubernetesCondition.description: A Kubernetes-style status condition reported by a controller.

status.description: Whether the condition is currently active.

reason.description: Machine-readable reason code for the current status.

message.description: Human-readable explanation of the current status.

lastTransitionTime.description: UTC timestamp of the last status transition.

KubernetesEvent.description: A Kubernetes event recorded against a resource (equivalent to `kubectl describe` Events).

reason.description: Short machine-readable reason for the event (e.g., 'Scheduled', 'Pulled', 'Failed').

age.description:
Human-readable elapsed time since the event last occurred
as reported by the Kubernetes API (e.g., '2m28s', '5h').

KubernetesGenericField.description:
A generic recursive field node used to represent resource-specific fields
that are not captured by the typed properties on KubernetesResourceSpec
or KubernetesResourceStatus (e.g., uncommon fields and CRD-specific data).

value.description: The string value of the field.

KubernetesLabel.description: A single Kubernetes label (key/value pair used for selection and grouping).

KubernetesObjectMeta.description: Metadata common to all Kubernetes objects (subset of ObjectMeta).

name.description: Name of the Kubernetes object, unique within its namespace.

namespaceName.description:
Namespace the object belongs to.
Empty for cluster-scoped resources such as Node and Namespace.

uid.description: Immutable UID assigned by the Kubernetes API server.

resourceVersion.description:
Opaque version string used for optimistic concurrency.
Clients must treat this as opaque and pass it unmodified on updates.

generation.description: Sequence number incremented each time the object spec changes.

creationTimestamp.description: UTC timestamp when the object was created.

deletionTimestamp.description:
UTC timestamp set when a graceful deletion has been requested.
Absent when the object is not being deleted.

labels.description: User-defined key/value pairs used for selection and grouping.

annotations.description: Key/value pairs for non-identifying auxiliary metadata.

ownerReferences.description: References to the objects that own this object (e.g., the ReplicaSet that owns a Pod).

finalizers.description: Named lifecycle hooks that must be resolved before the object is deleted.

KubernetesResource.description: Singleton container resource grouping cluster inventory reports.

KubernetesResourceListResult.description: The response of a KubernetesResource list operation.

value.description: The KubernetesResource items on this page

nextLink.description: The link to the next page of items

KubernetesResourceProperties.description:
Properties of a single KubernetesResources ARM resource.
Each ARM resource represents one Kubernetes object from the cluster inventory.
The ARM resource name encodes the kind, Kubernetes name, and namespace
(e.g., 'pod-nginx-6d4cf56db6-x7q2p-default', 'upgrade-assessment-current-kube-system').

apiVersion.description:
Kubernetes API version of the represented resource
(e.g., 'v1', 'apps/v1', 'upgrade.config.io/v1alpha1').

spec.description: Desired state of the Kubernetes resource.

status.description: Observed state of the Kubernetes resource, written by the controller.

events.description: Kubernetes events recorded against this resource.

attachedToExtension.description: Name of the extension this inventory resource is attached to.

KubernetesResourceSpec.description:
Desired state (spec) of a Kubernetes resource.

Well-known fields for native workloads (Pod, Deployment, Service, etc.) are typed
directly. Fields not covered by any typed property can be placed in additionalFields.

nodeName.description: Name of the node the pod is scheduled on. Populated for Pod resources.

restartPolicy.description: Pod restart policy (e.g., 'Always', 'OnFailure', 'Never'). Populated for Pod resources.

containers.description:
Containers belonging to the pod or pod template.
Populated for Pod, Deployment, StatefulSet, and DaemonSet resources.

initContainers.description:
Init containers that run before app containers start.
Populated for Pod, Deployment, StatefulSet, and DaemonSet resources.

terminationGracePeriodSeconds.description: Grace period in seconds before forcibly terminating the pod on deletion.

nodeSelector.description:
Node label selector constraining which nodes this pod may be scheduled on.
Populated for Pod, Deployment, StatefulSet, and DaemonSet resources.

priorityClassName.description:
Name of the PriorityClass governing scheduling priority and preemption for this pod.
Populated for Pod, Deployment, StatefulSet, and DaemonSet resources.

hostNetwork.description:
When true, the pod uses the host network namespace (disables pod network isolation).
Populated for Pod resources.

hostPid.description: When true, the pod shares the host PID namespace. Populated for Pod resources.

hostIpc.description: When true, the pod shares the host IPC namespace. Populated for Pod resources.

runtimeClassName.description:
Name of the RuntimeClass for sandbox or specialized container runtimes
(e.g., 'gvisor', 'kata-containers'). Populated for Pod resources.

imagePullSecrets.description:
Names of Secrets used to authenticate image pulls from private registries.
Populated for Pod, Deployment, StatefulSet, and DaemonSet resources.

podOs.description:
Operating system of the containers ('linux' or 'windows').
Populated for Pod resources.

replicas.description: Desired number of pod replicas. Populated for Deployment and StatefulSet resources.

serviceName.description:
Headless service name governing StatefulSet pod network identity.
Populated for StatefulSet resources.

selector.description:
Label selector identifying which pods are managed by this workload.
Populated for Deployment, StatefulSet, DaemonSet, and ReplicaSet resources.

maxUnavailable.description:
Maximum number of pods that can be unavailable during a rolling update
(absolute number or percentage string, e.g., '25%').
Populated for Deployment and DaemonSet resources.

maxSurge.description:
Maximum number of pods that can be created above the desired count during a rolling update
(absolute number or percentage string, e.g., '25%').
Populated for Deployment resources.

paused.description: When true, the Deployment rollout is paused. Populated for Deployment resources.

podManagementPolicy.description:
Pod creation and deletion ordering policy for a StatefulSet
('OrderedReady' for sequential or 'Parallel' for simultaneous).
Populated for StatefulSet resources.

volumeClaimTemplates.description:
PVC templates stamped out per StatefulSet replica to provide stable per-pod storage.
Populated for StatefulSet resources.

maxUnavailableReplicas.description:
Maximum number of pods that can be unavailable during a StatefulSet rolling update
(absolute number or percentage string, e.g., '1', '25%'). Beta as of Kubernetes 1.24.
Populated for StatefulSet resources.

serviceType.description:
How the service is exposed (e.g., 'ClusterIP', 'NodePort', 'LoadBalancer').
Populated for Service resources.

clusterIp.description: Cluster-internal IP assigned to the service. Populated for Service resources.

servicePorts.description: Ports exposed by the Service. Populated for Service resources.

serviceSelector.description:
Label selector routing traffic to pods backing this Service.
Populated for Service resources.

externalIps.description: External IP addresses accepted by cluster nodes for this Service. Populated for Service resources.

externalName.description: DNS CNAME alias target for ExternalName Services. Populated for Service resources.

externalTrafficPolicy.description:
Traffic routing policy for externally-facing addresses ('Cluster' or 'Local').
Populated for Service resources.

loadBalancerClass.description: Load balancer implementation class identifier. Populated for LoadBalancer Service resources.

loadBalancerIp.description:
Static IP address requested from the cloud provider for a LoadBalancer Service.
Deprecated in Kubernetes 1.24 in favor of spec.loadBalancerIP on the Service.
Populated for LoadBalancer Service resources.

serviceAccountName.description:
Name of the ServiceAccount the pod runs as.
Populated for Pod, Deployment, StatefulSet, and DaemonSet resources.

tolerations.description:
Tolerations allowing the pod to be scheduled on nodes with matching taints.
Populated for Pod, Deployment, StatefulSet, and DaemonSet resources.

volumes.description:
Volumes available to containers in the pod or pod template.
Populated for Pod, Deployment, StatefulSet, and DaemonSet resources.

dnsPolicy.description:
DNS policy for the pod (e.g., 'ClusterFirst', 'Default', 'None').
Populated for Pod resources.

securityContext.description:
Security context applied at the pod level.
Populated for Pod, Deployment, StatefulSet, and DaemonSet resources.

completions.description:
Total successful pod completions required for the Job to succeed.
Populated for Job resources.

parallelism.description:
Maximum number of pods running concurrently for a Job.
Populated for Job resources.

backoffLimit.description:
Number of retries before marking the Job as failed.
Populated for Job resources.

schedule.description:
Cron schedule expression.
Populated for CronJob resources.

concurrencyPolicy.description:
Concurrency policy for simultaneous CronJob runs ('Allow', 'Forbid', or 'Replace').
Populated for CronJob resources.

suspend.description:
When true, future executions are suspended.
Populated for CronJob and Job resources.

activeDeadlineSeconds.description:
Maximum duration in seconds before the Job is terminated regardless of its success.
Populated for Job resources.

completionMode.description:
Completion tracking mode for Job pods: 'NonIndexed' (default) or 'Indexed'.
Populated for Job resources.

ttlSecondsAfterFinished.description:
Seconds after Job completion before the Job and its pods are deleted automatically.
Populated for Job resources.

timeZone.description:
IANA time zone name for CronJob scheduling (e.g., 'America/New_York', 'UTC').
Populated for CronJob resources.

startingDeadlineSeconds.description:
Deadline in seconds for a CronJob run to start if missed; missed runs are ignored beyond this window.
Populated for CronJob resources.

accessModes.description:
Storage access modes requested or granted
(e.g., 'ReadWriteOnce', 'ReadOnlyMany', 'ReadWriteMany').
Populated for PersistentVolumeClaim and PersistentVolume resources.

storageClassName.description:
Name of the StorageClass for dynamic provisioning or PVC-to-PV binding.
Populated for PVC, PV, and StorageClass resources.

storageRequest.description:
Storage quantity requested by a PVC (e.g., '10Gi').
Populated for PersistentVolumeClaim resources.

storageCapacity.description:
Total storage capacity of a PV (e.g., '100Gi').
Populated for PersistentVolume resources.

reclaimPolicy.description:
Reclaim policy for a PV after its claim is released ('Retain', 'Delete', or 'Recycle').
Populated for PersistentVolume and StorageClass resources.

volumeMode.description:
Volume mode describing how the volume is used ('Filesystem' or 'Block').
Populated for PVC and PV resources.

boundVolumeName.description:
For a PVC: the name of the PV it is bound to.
For a PV: the name of the PVC bound to it.

claimRefNamespace.description:
Namespace of the PVC bound to a PV (populated in PV claimRef).
Populated for PersistentVolume resources.

csiDriver.description:
CSI driver name managing this PersistentVolume (e.g., 'disk.csi.azure.com').
Populated for PersistentVolume resources backed by a CSI driver.

csiVolumeHandle.description:
Unique volume handle assigned by the CSI driver.
Populated for PersistentVolume resources backed by a CSI driver.

provisioner.description:
CSI driver (provisioner) name for a StorageClass (e.g., 'disk.csi.azure.com').
Populated for StorageClass resources.

allowVolumeExpansion.description:
When true, PVCs using this StorageClass may be expanded after provisioning.
Populated for StorageClass resources.

secretType.description:
Kubernetes Secret type (e.g., 'Opaque', 'kubernetes.io/tls', 'kubernetes.io/service-account-token').
Populated for Secret resources.

configMapData.description:
Key-value pairs stored in the ConfigMap (e.g., configuration files, environment values).
Each entry maps a string key to a string value.
Populated for ConfigMap resources.

configMapBinaryDataKeys.description:
Keys present in the ConfigMap binaryData field.
Binary values are not captured; only key names are stored for reference.
Populated for ConfigMap resources.

configMapImmutable.description:
When true, the ConfigMap is immutable and cannot be updated after creation.
Populated for ConfigMap resources.

automountServiceAccountToken.description:
When false, prevents the default Kubernetes service account token from being
auto-mounted into pods running as this ServiceAccount.
Populated for ServiceAccount resources.

attacherName.description:
Name of the CSI driver that should attach/detach the volume (e.g., 'disk.csi.azure.com').
Populated for VolumeAttachment resources.

attachedVolumeName.description:
Name of the PersistentVolume being attached.
Populated for VolumeAttachment resources.

ingressClassName.description:
Name of the IngressClass that governs this Ingress resource.
Populated for Ingress resources.

ingressRules.description:
Routing rules mapping hostnames and URL paths to backend Services.
Populated for Ingress resources.

ingressTls.description:
TLS termination configuration for Ingress resources.
Populated for Ingress resources.

defaultBackendServiceName.description:
Backend Service name to route traffic when no rule matches.
Populated for Ingress resources.

defaultBackendServicePort.description:
Backend Service port to route traffic when no rule matches (number or named port).
Populated for Ingress resources.

ingressController.description:
Controller class reference for an IngressClass (e.g., 'nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/controller').
Populated for IngressClass resources.

podSelectorLabels.description:
Label selector identifying the pods this NetworkPolicy applies to.
Populated for NetworkPolicy resources.

networkPolicyTypes.description:
Policy directions enforced ('Ingress', 'Egress', or both).
Populated for NetworkPolicy resources.

rbacRules.description:
RBAC permission rules granting access to Kubernetes API resources.
Populated for Role and ClusterRole resources.

rbacSubjects.description:
Subjects (users, groups, service accounts) granted permissions by this binding.
Populated for RoleBinding and ClusterRoleBinding resources.

rbacRoleRefKind.description:
Kind of the role referenced by a binding ('Role' or 'ClusterRole').
Populated for RoleBinding and ClusterRoleBinding resources.

rbacRoleRefName.description:
Name of the role referenced by a binding.
Populated for RoleBinding and ClusterRoleBinding resources.

scaleTargetRefKind.description:
Kind of the resource being autoscaled (e.g., 'Deployment', 'StatefulSet').
Populated for HorizontalPodAutoscaler resources.

scaleTargetRefName.description:
Name of the resource being autoscaled.
Populated for HorizontalPodAutoscaler resources.

minReplicas.description:
Minimum replica count for autoscaling.
Populated for HorizontalPodAutoscaler resources.

maxReplicas.description:
Maximum replica count for autoscaling.
Populated for HorizontalPodAutoscaler resources.

minAvailable.description:
Minimum number of pods that must remain available during voluntary disruptions
(absolute number or percentage string, e.g., '2', '50%').
Populated for PodDisruptionBudget resources.

maxUnavailablePods.description:
Maximum number of pods that may be simultaneously unavailable during voluntary disruptions.
Populated for PodDisruptionBudget resources.

priorityValue.description:
Numeric scheduling priority value for a PriorityClass.
Populated for PriorityClass resources.

globalDefault.description:
When true, this PriorityClass is the cluster default for pods without an explicit priorityClassName.
Populated for PriorityClass resources.

preemptionPolicy.description:
Preemption policy for pods with this priority class when system resources are scarce
('PreemptLowerPriority' or 'Never').
Populated for PriorityClass resources.

taints.description:
Taints applied to the node, repelling pods that lack a matching toleration.
Populated for Node resources.

unschedulable.description:
When true, the node is cordoned and will not accept new pod scheduling.
Populated for Node resources.

providerId.description:
Cloud provider identifier for the node
(e.g., 'azure:///subscriptions/.../virtualMachines/vm-0').
Populated for Node resources.

podCidr.description:
IP range assigned to pods running on this node.
Populated for Node resources.

endpointSliceAddressType.description:
Address family for all endpoints in this EndpointSlice ('IPv4', 'IPv6', or 'FQDN').
Populated for EndpointSlice resources.

endpointSliceEndpoints.description:
Endpoints contained in this EndpointSlice.
Populated for EndpointSlice resources.

endpointSlicePorts.description:
Ports exposed by the endpoints in this EndpointSlice.
Populated for EndpointSlice resources.

csrSignerName.description:
Name of the signer that will sign the certificate (e.g., 'kubernetes.io/kube-apiserver-client').
Populated for CertificateSigningRequest resources.

csrUsername.description:
Name of the user that created the CertificateSigningRequest.
Populated for CertificateSigningRequest resources.

csrUsages.description:
Key usages requested for the certificate (e.g., 'client auth', 'server auth', 'digital signature').
Populated for CertificateSigningRequest resources.

csrExpirationSeconds.description:
Requested duration in seconds for the issued certificate.
Populated for CertificateSigningRequest resources.

storageCapacityAvailable.description:
Available storage capacity for the StorageClass in this topology segment (e.g., '500Gi').
Populated for CSIStorageCapacity resources.

maximumVolumeSize.description:
Maximum size of a single volume that can be created in this topology segment.
Populated for CSIStorageCapacity resources.

admissionPolicyFailurePolicy.description:
What happens when a policy evaluation fails: 'Fail' rejects the request; 'Ignore' allows it.
Populated for ValidatingAdmissionPolicy resources.

admissionPolicyMatchConstraints.description:
API resource rules that trigger this ValidatingAdmissionPolicy
(e.g., 'CREATE pods', 'UPDATE deployments').
Each entry is a human-readable summary of a resource rule.
Populated for ValidatingAdmissionPolicy resources.

admissionPolicyBindingPolicyName.description:
Name of the ValidatingAdmissionPolicy this binding applies.
Populated for ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding resources.

admissionPolicyValidationActions.description:
How validation failures are enforced: 'Deny', 'Warn', or 'Audit'.
Populated for ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding resources.

additionalFields.description:
Additional resource-specific fields not represented by the typed properties above.
Used for uncommon fields and custom resource definition instances.

KubernetesResourceStatus.description:
Observed state (status) of a Kubernetes resource, written by the controller.

Well-known fields for native workloads (Pod, Deployment, Service, etc.) are typed
directly. Fields not covered by any typed property can be placed in additionalFields.

conditions.description: Conditions reported by the controller managing this resource.

message.description: Human-readable message summarizing the resource state.

replicas.description: Total non-terminating pods targeted by this workload. Populated for Deployment, StatefulSet, ReplicaSet.

readyReplicas.description: Number of pods ready to serve requests. Populated for Deployment, StatefulSet, DaemonSet.

availableReplicas.description: Number of pods available for at least minReadySeconds. Populated for Deployment, DaemonSet, StatefulSet.

updatedReplicas.description: Pods running the current (desired) template spec. Populated for Deployment, StatefulSet, DaemonSet.

unavailableReplicas.description: Pods required but not yet available. Populated for Deployment resources.

currentReplicas.description:
Pods running the previous revision (before update completes).
Populated for StatefulSet resources.

currentRevision.description:
Name of the StatefulSet revision corresponding to currentReplicas.
Populated for StatefulSet resources.

updateRevision.description:
Name of the StatefulSet revision applied to updatedReplicas.
Populated for StatefulSet resources.

desiredReplicas.description:
Desired replica count calculated by the HorizontalPodAutoscaler.
Populated for HorizontalPodAutoscaler resources.

terminatingReplicas.description:
Pods that are terminating but not yet removed from the endpoints.
Populated for Deployment and ReplicaSet resources.

desiredNumberScheduled.description: Total nodes that should run the daemon pod. Populated for DaemonSet resources.

currentNumberScheduled.description: Nodes running at least one daemon pod. Populated for DaemonSet resources.

numberReady.description: Nodes with a Ready daemon pod. Populated for DaemonSet resources.

numberMisscheduled.description:
Nodes running the daemon pod that should not be running it.
Populated for DaemonSet resources.

numberUnavailable.description: Nodes missing an available daemon pod. Populated for DaemonSet resources.

podIp.description: IP address allocated to the pod within the cluster. Populated for Pod resources.

podIps.description: Additional pod IPs assigned in multi-network or dual-stack clusters. Populated for Pod resources.

hostIp.description: IP address of the node hosting the pod. Populated for Pod resources.

hostIps.description: Additional host IPs for dual-stack nodes. Populated for Pod resources.

startTime.description: UTC timestamp when the pod was accepted by the scheduler. Populated for Pod resources.

containerStatuses.description:
Runtime status of each container in the pod.
Populated for Pod resources.

initContainerStatuses.description:
Runtime status of each init container in the pod.
Populated for Pod resources.

qosClass.description:
QoS class assigned by Kubernetes based on resource requests and limits.
One of 'Guaranteed', 'Burstable', or 'BestEffort'. Populated for Pod resources.

reason.description:
Brief CamelCase reason for the pod's current state (e.g., 'Evicted', 'OOMKilled').
Populated for Pod resources.

observedGeneration.description:
Most recent generation observed by the controller.
Populated for Deployment, StatefulSet, DaemonSet, and Pod resources.

activeJobCount.description: Number of currently active (running or pending) pods. Populated for Job resources.

succeededJobCount.description: Number of pods that completed successfully. Populated for Job resources.

failedJobCount.description: Number of pods that failed. Populated for Job resources.

readyPods.description: Number of pods in ready state for a Job. Populated for Job resources.

jobStartTime.description: UTC timestamp when the Job started executing. Populated for Job resources.

jobCompletionTime.description: UTC timestamp when the Job completed successfully. Populated for Job resources.

lastScheduleTime.description: UTC timestamp when the CronJob last successfully scheduled a Job.

lastSuccessfulTime.description: UTC timestamp when the last Job triggered by this CronJob completed successfully.

loadBalancerIngress.description:
External IP addresses or hostnames assigned to LoadBalancer Services or Ingress resources.
Populated for Service (type LoadBalancer) and Ingress resources.

lastScaleTime.description:
UTC timestamp of the most recent scaling event triggered by the HPA.
Populated for HorizontalPodAutoscaler resources.

nodeAddresses.description:
Network addresses of the node (InternalIP, ExternalIP, Hostname).
Populated for Node resources.

nodeInfo.description:
Operating system and runtime information for the node.
Populated for Node resources.

nodeCapacity.description:
Total hardware resources of the node (cpu, memory, pods, ephemeral-storage).
Populated for Node resources.

nodeAllocatable.description:
Resources available for scheduling after system reservation.
Populated for Node resources.

volumesAttached.description:
Volumes that have been successfully attached to the node by the attach/detach controller.
Populated for Node resources.

currentHealthy.description: Currently healthy (Ready) pods counted by the PodDisruptionBudget.

desiredHealthy.description: Minimum healthy pods required by the PodDisruptionBudget.

disruptionsAllowed.description: Number of pod disruptions currently allowed by the PodDisruptionBudget.

expectedPods.description: Total number of pods counted by the PodDisruptionBudget (ready + not ready).

pvcCapacity.description:
Actual provisioned storage capacity for the PVC after binding (e.g., '10Gi').
Populated for PersistentVolumeClaim resources.

pvcAccessModes.description:
Access modes granted to the PVC after it is bound to a PV.
Populated for PersistentVolumeClaim resources.

volumeAttached.description:
When true, the volume is successfully attached to the target node.
Populated for VolumeAttachment resources.

additionalFields.description:
Additional resource-specific status fields not represented by the typed properties above.
Used for uncommon fields and custom resource definition instances.

NetworkProtocol.description: Network protocol used by a container port.

NodeAddress.description: A network address associated with a Kubernetes Node.

address.description: The address value (IP address or hostname).

NodeResourceMap.description: Resource quantities reported for a Kubernetes Node (capacity or allocatable).

pods.description: Maximum number of pods the node supports.

NodeSystemInfo.description: Operating system and runtime information reported by the kubelet for a Node.

kernelVersion.description: Kernel version of the node OS (e.g., '5.15.0-1041-azure').

kubeletVersion.description: Kubelet binary version running on the node (e.g., 'v1.29.2').

kubeProxyVersion.description: kube-proxy version running on the node.

NodeTaint.description: A taint applied to a Kubernetes Node that repels pods without a matching toleration.

value.description: Taint value matched by tolerations using the 'Equal' operator.

NodeTaintEffect.description: Effect applied to pods that do not tolerate a node taint.

values.name: NoSchedule
values.description: New pods will not be scheduled on the tainted node.

values.name: PreferNoSchedule
values.description: The scheduler tries to avoid placing new pods on the tainted node.

values.name: NoExecute
values.description: Existing pods without a matching toleration are evicted from the tainted node.

OwnerReference.description: A reference from a Kubernetes object to its owner (e.g., a ReplicaSet owning a Pod).

controller.description: True when this owner manages the lifecycle of the referencing object.

blockOwnerDeletion.description: If true, deletion of the owner is blocked until this reference is removed.

PodSecurityContext.description: Security context applied at the pod level, affecting all containers.

fsGroup.description: GID of the owning group for volume mounts; processes also run as this GID.

runAsUser.description: UID to run all containers as. Overridden per-container by ContainerSecurityContext.runAsUser.

runAsGroup.description: GID to run all containers as.

runAsNonRoot.description: Require all containers to run as non-root when true.

supplementalGroups.description: Supplemental GIDs applied to each container in addition to the primary GID.

seccompProfile.description: Seccomp profile type applied to all containers ('RuntimeDefault', 'Unconfined', or 'Localhost').

appArmorProfile.description: AppArmor profile type applied to all containers ('RuntimeDefault', 'Unconfined', or 'Localhost').

PolicyRule.description: A single RBAC permission rule granting verbs on Kubernetes API resources.

apiGroups.description: API groups the rule applies to (use '' for the core group).

resources.description: Resource types the rule applies to (e.g., 'pods', 'deployments', 'secrets').

resourceNames.description: Specific resource names this rule applies to. Empty means all instances.

RbacSubject.description: A subject (user, group, or service account) in a RoleBinding or ClusterRoleBinding.

namespaceName.description: Namespace of the ServiceAccount. Required when kind is ServiceAccount.

ServicePort.description: A port exposed by a Kubernetes Service.

port.description: Port number the Service listens on (cluster-internal).

targetPort.description: Port or named port on the target pod the traffic is forwarded to.

nodePort.description: NodePort allocated for NodePort and LoadBalancer Services.

name.description: Optional name for this port, unique within the Service.

Toleration.description: A toleration allows a pod to be scheduled on a node with a matching taint.

key.description: Taint key the toleration applies to. Empty matches all keys.

operator.description:
Operator to use when matching: 'Equal' matches when key and value both match;
'Exists' matches when the key is present regardless of value.

value.description: Taint value matched when operator is 'Equal'.

effect.description:
Taint effect this toleration applies to
('NoSchedule', 'PreferNoSchedule', or 'NoExecute'). Empty matches all effects.

tolerationSeconds.description: Seconds a pod tolerates a NoExecute taint before being evicted.

Volume.description:
A volume available to containers in a pod.
The sourceType field identifies the kind of volume source
(e.g., 'emptyDir', 'configMap', 'secret', 'persistentVolumeClaim', 'projected').

name.description: Name of the volume, referenced by container volumeMounts.

source.description: Primary reference for the volume source (e.g., ConfigMap name or PVC name).

sizeLimit.description: Storage size limit for emptyDir volumes (e.g., '50Mi').

VolumeClaimTemplate.description: A PersistentVolumeClaim template in a StatefulSet that provisions stable storage per replica.

name.description: Name of the volume claim template, referenced by container volumeMounts.

VolumeMount.description: A volume mount inside a container.

name.description: Name matching a volume declared in the pod spec.

mountPath.description: Absolute path inside the container where the volume is mounted.

readOnly.description: Mount the volume as read-only when true.

subPath.description: Sub-path within the volume to mount instead of the volume root.
